task_description: A severe outage affected all four services starting at 03:22 UTC during a maintenance window. The incident lasted 25 minutes and exhibited unusual characteristics: neither the most recent deploy NOR the concurrent infrastructure event alone can explain the failure. The root cause is a COMBINATION of a specific commit and a specific infrastructure event that interacted non-obviously. Identify BOTH contributing causes AND reconstruct the complete causal chain.

frontend.message: Deployment v2.16.0 rolled out — new caching layer for API responses
Maintenance window started. Traffic shifted to secondary region.
Auth service unreachable on primary endpoint. Attempting failover to secondary.
Secondary auth endpoint also failing — both AZs affected
Complete auth failure. All API requests returning 503.
Static page fallback activated. Dynamic content unavailable.
DNS resolution for auth-service.internal intermittently failing
Deployment v2.15.9 rolled out — bug fix for pagination
Deployment v2.15.8 rolled out — performance monitoring improvements
Some requests succeeding through cached routes — inconsistent availability
Auth service partially recovering in AZ-b

auth.message: Deployment v1.10.0 rolled out — unified connection manager for multi-AZ failover
Maintenance window: pre-check passed. Multi-AZ failover configured.
Network latency to data-service AZ-a spiking: 450ms avg (normal: 2ms)
Connection manager initiating failover from AZ-a to AZ-b for data-service
Failover failed: AZ-b endpoint for data-service returned ECONNREFUSED
Connection manager bug: failover closes ALL connections before opening new ones. Now have 0 active connections.
Cannot reconnect to data-service on ANY AZ. Connection manager in broken state.
Auth service completely down — no data-service connectivity
Connection manager retry loop: attempt 15 — still ECONNREFUSED on AZ-b, timeout on AZ-a
Deployment v1.9.9 rolled out — added structured logging
Manual restart of auth-service. Connection manager re-initialized.
Data-service connectivity restored via AZ-a (network maintenance completed)

data.message: Deployment v3.4.0 rolled out — added AZ-b read replica support
Deployment v3.4.1 rolled out — hot-patch for AZ-b endpoint registration
Maintenance window started. AZ-a network maintenance scheduled.
Network interface eth0 experiencing packet loss: 15% on AZ-a subnet
Database primary in AZ-a responding slowly — query latency 10x normal
Connections from auth-service timing out after 5000ms
AZ-b read replica endpoint not registered in service discovery. Hot-patch v3.4.1 only registered AZ-a endpoint.
No healthy endpoints available: AZ-a degraded by network maintenance, AZ-b not registered
Service effectively down — all connection attempts failing
Deployment v3.3.9 rolled out — query optimization for batch lookups
Deployment v3.4.2 rolled out — increased connection timeout from 3s to 5s
Deployment v3.4.3 rolled out — added health check for AZ-b endpoint
Batch service reporting read latency increase but still functional
AZ-a network recovering — packet loss down to 2%
Manual registration of AZ-b endpoint in service discovery

batch.message: Deployment v1.1.0 rolled out — improved batch job monitoring
Some batch jobs retrying due to slow reads
Batch processing continuing with degraded performance

spans.error: network timeout AZ-a, not registered AZ-b
connection manager in broken state — 0 active connections
high network latency to DB primary in AZ-a
failover procedure: closed 48 AZ-a connections, attempted AZ-b, got ECONNREFUSED, now at 0 total connections

commits.author: carol@company.com
commits.message: feat: unified connection manager for multi-AZ failover — closes old connections before opening new ones to prevent connection leaks

commits.author: alice@company.com
commits.message: feat: add AZ-b read replica support for improved availability

commits.author: alice@company.com
commits.message: hotfix: register AZ-b endpoint in service discovery — ONLY registers AZ-a due to typo in config template
commits.diff:
--- a/data-service/discovery/register.go
+++ b/data-service/discovery/register.go
+func RegisterEndpoints() {
+    // Register primary AZ-a
+    discovery.Register("data-service", "az-a", primaryEndpoint)
+    // TODO: Also register AZ-b — currently skipped due to config template issue
+    // discovery.Register("data-service", "az-b", replicaEndpoint)
+    log.Info("Registered data-service endpoints") // misleading: only AZ-a registered
+}

config_changes.description: Enabled AZ-b read replica (code deployment pending)
Close existing connections before failover to prevent connection leaks
Added AZ-b to service discovery config (but code only registers AZ-a)
Increased DB pool size in preparation for AZ-b
Increased auth timeout for maintenance window
Reduced batch concurrency for maintenance window
Increased acceptable replica lag for maintenance
Increased retry attempts for maintenance resilience

infra_events.description: Scheduled network maintenance on AZ-a subnet 10.0.1.0/24 started. Expected duration: 45 minutes. Gradual traffic rerouting.
AZ-a network degradation detected: packet loss 15%, latency spike 450ms on subnet 10.0.1.0/24. Affects data-service primary.
Network maintenance completed. AZ-a subnet restored to normal operation.
Pre-maintenance scaling: added 2 extra pods per service for resilience.
DNS records updated for multi-AZ endpoints. TTL set to 60s.

ground_truth.explanation: The auth service connection manager (commit-auth200) had a bug: on failover it closes ALL connections before opening new ones. The AZ-a network degradation (infra-202) triggered a failover attempt. The failover target AZ-b was unavailable because commit-data201 had a bug that only registered AZ-a in service discovery. Result: auth closed all AZ-a connections, couldn't open AZ-b connections, left with 0 connections and no recovery path.
